VOC Taxonomy: Rep Was Courteous → Customer explicitly describes the representative’s positive demeanor (e.g., polite, friendly, respectful, professional, patient) during the interaction.
Rep Not Courteous → Customer explicitly describes the representative as discourteous (e.g., rude, dismissive, disrespectful, hostile, pushy, unprofessional) in tone or behavior.
Rep Was Helpful → Customer states the representative provided effective assistance, support, or problem-solving (e.g., helped, resolved, accommodated, went above and beyond).
Rep Not Helpful → Customer states the representative failed to provide useful assistance (e.g., unresponsive, no help, wasted time, didn’t answer questions, incapable of resolving).
Rep Was Knowledgeable → Customer indicates the representative was competent and informed, giving confident guidance or accurate explanations (e.g., knowledgeable, well-trained, knew their stuff).
Rep Not Knowledgeable → Customer indicates the representative lacked knowledge or competence (e.g., clueless, untrained, inexperienced, misinformed, didn’t understand the issue).
Bill Increase → Customer comments that their bill increased compared to a prior period (e.g., bill went up, unexpected hike, rate increased, higher than last month).
Bill Too High → Customer expresses that the bill is too expensive overall (e.g., overpriced, paying too much, excessive charges) without requiring a comparison to prior bills.
Bill Dispute → Customer challenges specific charges or claims a billing error and requests investigation, correction, or refund (e.g., incorrect charge, wrong amount, dispute).
Seeking Credits → Customer requests an account credit/adjustment/refund/compensation to be applied due to an error, overcharge, or service issue (i.e., a credit request rather than a promo).
Need to Lower Bill → Customer states a need to reduce ongoing monthly cost for affordability/value reasons (e.g., can’t afford, want cheaper plan, reduce charges), not primarily to correct an error.
Call Disconnects → Customer reports the call with the representative was unexpectedly disconnected/dropped/ended abruptly during the interaction.
Transfers/Multiple Reps → Customer reports interacting with more than one representative, including being transferred or passed between agents during the same issue/contact.
Long Wait for Rep → Customer complains about a long delay before reaching any representative (queue time prior to first connection), not hold time after connection.
Long Hold Time → Customer complains about being placed on hold for a long time during the call after already speaking with a representative.
Multiple Holds → Customer reports being put on hold multiple separate times within the same call (frequency), regardless of the length of each hold.
Wait/Hold Time → Customer mentions long waiting/hold time in general without clearly separating whether it occurred before reaching a rep or while on hold mid-call.

Callbacks Never Received → Customer explicitly states a promised callback (agent/supervisor/manager follow-up) did not occur after a disconnection, escalation, or scheduled return call, or that the automated callback system failed (e.g., called and hung up, timed out).

Missed Field Tech Appointment → Customer reports a scheduled technician visit (install/repair/service call) was a no-show or missed appointment, often with no advance notice/communication and sometimes repeated reschedules.

Wireless Coverage → Customer reports poor or no mobile network coverage/signal in their area (e.g., no bars/no service/SOS only/dead zone, recurring outages, must move to a specific spot to get reception) impacting ability to use cellular service.

No Feedback → Verbatim provides no substantive feedback about the interaction (e.g., simple acknowledgments/declines such as 'no', 'none', 'n/a', 'nothing', 'no thanks', 'ok', 'done', 'thank you') or close variants.
Error Correction → Verbatim indicates the customer entered the wrong survey rating/response and is correcting it (e.g., 'I meant 5', 'should have been 5', 'mistake', 'sorry wrong score', '1 not 2').
Stop → Verbatim is an opt-out request intended to stop receiving SMS/surveys (e.g., 'stop', 'opt out', 'unsubscribe', 'end', 'do not text').

Wrong Customer → Verbatim states the survey was received in error because the respondent was not the person who interacted with AT&T (e.g., “I didn’t call”, “I didn’t visit a store”, “this is not [name]”, “this wasn’t me”). Exclude “wrong number” when it refers to pressing/entering the wrong survey rating/response (that belongs to Error Correction), unless the customer clearly says the survey was sent to the wrong person (identity mismatch).
